title:
Module 6
Growing Social Media
subtitle: Grow Your Social Medias: Instagram, Threads, Reddit, Twitter

blocks.content: Instagram is your primary traffic source. Everything else feeds into it or supports it. After warmup is complete (Module 5), you are ready to launch your real growth phase. This is where your page goes from looking human to actually growing.

items: Start Reels only AFTER warmup is fully complete
First Reel should be simple: face-focused, trending audio, 5-10 seconds
Post 3-5 Reels per week during the growth phase
Use trending sounds — check the Reels audio library daily
Keep captions short and personality-driven, not salesy
Reels — Primary discovery tool (reach new people)
Stories — Daily engagement (keep existing followers active)
Carousels — Profile building (make your grid look real)
Feed posts — Support content (fills out your page)

right.content: Instagram Content Execution

items: Instagram gets your BEST content first — always
Never post the same image on Instagram and another platform same day
Vary content: selfies, lifestyle, casual, glam — not all one type
Stories should feel raw and 'in the moment' even if AI-generated
Carousel posts: 3-5 images, first image is the hook

right.title: IMAGE PLACEHOLDER
right.content: Example Instagram grid layout showing content variety and posting pattern

right.title: Growth Trigger
right.content: The algorithm pushes Reels when early engagement is strong. Post when your audience is online (check Insights). The first 30 minutes determine if the Reel gets distributed or dies.

blocks.content: Threads is not a separate platform — it is a tool that feeds directly into your Instagram growth. Threads gives new pages reach without history. You use it to manufacture demand and curiosity, then push that attention to your Instagram profile.

left.content: Threads Phase 1: How to Post & Reply
Threads posts should make someone think: 'Who is this girl?' Not 'She is selling something.'

items: Post relatable, personality-driven text posts
Reply to trending threads with witty or relatable takes
Use your AI model's persona voice — casual, human, not corporate
Posts that trigger comments get pushed to more people
Post 3-5 times per day on Threads

left.content:
Thread post: 'accidentally made eye contact with someone at the gym and now we're married i guess'
Relatable, funny, conversation-starting.

Thread post: 'Check out my page for exclusive content! Link in bio 🔥'
Sales pitch. No one engages. Algorithm buries it.

right.content: Threads Phase 2: Scaling & Testing

items: Track which post styles get the most replies
Double down on formats that work (opinions, hot takes, relatable moments)
Test different posting times
Increase to 5-8 posts per day as engagement grows
Every Threads post that pops sends people to your IG profile

right.content: Thread Types That Work

items: Hot takes and opinions (generates replies)
Relatable daily moments
Questions that spark conversation
Slightly controversial (not offensive) statements
Personal stories that feel authentic

right.title: IMAGE PLACEHOLDER
right.content: Example Threads posts that drove Instagram profile visits

blocks.content: Reddit is not a main platform. It is a multiplier. Reddit works best as an opportunistic traffic source. Not as a main platform. But as a multiplier when a post hits the right audience.

Reddit is slow, strict, and heavily moderated. Most subreddits do not allow promotion and remove content quickly. But only if the account looks fake, rushed, and natural. A good Reddit account does not look like a model account. It looks like a normal user who belongs to a niche.

items: Username: cute or quirky, no sexual terms
Bio: short, personality-driven, no links during warmup
Avatar: use your AI model's face
The fan should feel like they discovered you inside their own community
Find niche subreddits that match your persona's aesthetic
Read each subreddit's rules before posting anything
Start with SFW subreddits only
Target subreddits with 50k-500k members (sweet spot for visibility)
Join 10-15 relevant subreddits

left.title: IMAGE PLACEHOLDER
left.content: Example subreddit list organized by niche type

right.content: Warmup & Karma Farming

items: Spend 7 days building karma before posting your own content
Comment on popular posts with genuine, helpful replies
Upvote and engage naturally
Post memes or relatable content in non-model subreddits
Need ~100 karma minimum before most subreddits allow image posts

right.content: Posting Phase

items: Post 1-2 images per subreddit per week maximum
Titles should be casual, not promotional: 'felt cute today' not 'check me out'
Respond to every comment on your posts
Rotate subreddits — don't spam the same one
One viral Reddit post can drive hundreds of profile visits

right.title: Reddit Rule
right.content: Reddit users hate self-promotion. If your account looks like it exists only to post images, you will be downvoted and banned. Blend in first, post second.

blocks.content: TikTok is a secondary traffic source. Instagram remains the main platform. TikTok converts worse than Instagram — estimated 5 to 10 times worse in CVR. Its value is extra reach when combined with other organic marketing.

items: Only post very safe content — TikTok moderation is stricter
Carousel/photo format is the safest content type
Recycle your best Instagram content (but not same day)
Short video clips (3-10 seconds) with trending sounds
Face-focused content outperforms everything else
Scroll and watch content for 15-20 min/day
Like and comment on other creators' posts
Reply to comments on your own posts
Post consistently: 1-2 posts per day
Never use banned sounds or effects

right.content: What TikTok Rewards

items: Accounts that look human and act slowly
Content that stays within platform comfort zones
Consistent posting schedule over time
High watch time and completion rate on videos

right.content: Expected Results
If done correctly, TikTok should add approximately 20% more subscribers to your overall funnel. It is not the main driver. It is a supplement. Treat it as leverage, not focus.

right.title: Safety Rule
right.content: If content feels risky, do not post it on TikTok. Safe posting beats frequent posting. One strike on TikTok can shadow your account for weeks.

right.title: IMAGE PLACEHOLDER
right.content: TikTok content examples: safe carousel format vs risky content that gets flagged

blocks.content: Twitter/X is a discovery platform, not a sales platform. It is built on replies, not posts. Your growth on Twitter comes from being seen in other people's conversations, not from broadcasting your own content. This is the opposite of Instagram.

items: Profile photo: best AI model face shot, high quality
Banner: lifestyle or aesthetic image of your persona
Bio: short, personality-driven, casual tone
Pinned tweet: your best-performing or most engaging post
No links in bio during warmup
Find popular accounts your target audience follows
Reply to their tweets with witty, relevant, engaging takes
Every reply is seen by that account's followers
Goal: make people click on your profile from your reply
Reply 10-20 times per day to build visibility

right.content: Target Audience
Twitter/X is strong for reaching affluent men aged 30-50+. This demographic spends more per subscriber than younger audiences. Target accounts and conversations that this audience follows.
Warmup (14 Days)

items: Days 1-3: Scroll, like, retweet. No original posts.
Days 4-7: Start replying to popular tweets. 5-10 replies/day.
Days 8-10: First original posts. Mix of text and images.
Days 11-14: Increase posting. Add images to tweets.
After 14 days: Full posting schedule, add link in bio.

right.title: Shadowban Check
right.content: Check your shadowban status regularly. If your replies stop getting likes, you may be shadowbanned. Reduce activity for 48 hours and resume slowly.

blocks.content: You are not growing on five separate platforms. You are running one system with multiple tools. Each platform serves a role. Coordination between them is what multiplies your results.

items: Instagram gets your best content FIRST — always
Never post the same image on two platforms on the same day
Threads content is text-only — don't duplicate Instagram captions
TikTok recycles Instagram content (delayed by 2-3 days minimum)
Reddit gets unique content tailored to each subreddit
Twitter gets a mix of text posts and image tweets
Instagram: 4-5 Reels + daily Stories + 2-3 carousels
Threads: 3-8 text posts per day
TikTok: 1-2 posts per day
Reddit: 3-5 posts per week across subreddits
Twitter: 2-3 tweets + 10-20 replies per day

right.content: Traffic Flow Between Platforms

items: Threads → drives profile visits to Instagram
Instagram → converts followers via link in bio
TikTok → supplements Instagram traffic
Reddit → niche viral spikes to profile
Twitter → steady discovery and brand presence
All platforms → Link in Bio → Telegram → Fanvue

right.title: IMAGE PLACEHOLDER
right.content: Cross-platform content calendar template showing weekly distribution across all 5 platforms

right.title: Coordination Rule
right.content: Your persona must feel like the same person across all platforms. Same face, same name, same personality. The only thing that changes is the content format and tone for each platform.

summary_points: Instagram is the primary platform — Reels drive discovery, Stories drive engagement
Threads manufactures demand that feeds directly into Instagram growth
Reddit is a multiplier, not a main platform — blend in before posting
TikTok is secondary reach — only safe content, carousel format is safest
Twitter/X grows through replies, not broadcasting — target affluent audiences
Coordinate content across platforms — same persona, different formats, never same day
Instagram always gets your best content first
